page.content: In the North American market, Starbucks holds a market share of 30% with total revenue of $3.5B, net income of $1.2B, CAPEX of $0.5B, and a year-over-year growth of 5.5%.

lines.text: In the North American market, Starbucks holds a market share of 30% with total revenue of $3.5B, net income of $1.2B,
CAPEX of $0.5B, and a year-over-year growth of 5.5%.

page.content: Within this market, Canada contributes a 15% share, generating $1.2B in revenue, $0.4B in net income, $0.2B in CAPEX, and a growth rate of 5.0%. The USA accounts for 25% of the market share, with revenues of $2.3B, net income of $0.8B, CAPEX of $0.3B, and a growth rate of 5.7%. Mexico, with a 5% share, has $0.2B in revenue and $0.05B in net income. Yearly data shows market shares of 30% in 2019 with $3.0B revenue, $1.0B net income, and $0.4B CAPEX; 28% in 2020 with $3.1B revenue and $1.1B net income; 29% in 2021 with $3.2B revenue, $1.15B net income, and $0.48B CAPEX; 31% in 2022 with $3.4B revenue, $1.2B net income, and $0.5B CAPEX; and 30% in 2023 with $3.5B revenue, $1.25B net income, and $0.52B CAPEX. Future projections indicate a market share of 32% in 2024 with revenues of $3.7B, $1.3B net income, and $0.55B CAPEX, 33% in 2025 with $3.9B revenue, $1.4B net income, and $0.6B CAPEX, and 34% in 2026 with $4.1B revenue, $1.5B net income, and $0.65B CAPEX. In the product launch overview for 2023, Starbucks sold 1,200,000 units generating $15M in revenue with 5% sales growth at an average price of $12.50. For 2024, 1,500,000 units are projected, yielding $20M with 8% sales growth at an average price of $13.33.

page.content: In 2025, sales are expected to reach 2,000,000 units with $30M in revenue and a 10% growth rate at $15.00 average price. The 2026 forecast anticipates 2,500,000 units sold, generating $40M with 12% growth at an average price of $16.00. By 2027, sales are projected at 3,000,000 units with $50M revenue and 15% growth at an average price of $16.67, totaling 10,500,000 units sold over the period.
